  Type '[number, number, number]' is not assignable to type '[number, number]'.
    Source has 3 element(s) but target allows only 2.
tluaTupleTypes.tlua(85,34): error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '3' does not exist in type 'SparseU'.


==== tluaTupleTypes.tlua (21 errors) ====
    // Tuple types `[A, B]` in .tlua source: a fixed-shape table whose elements
    // live at the 1-based Lua sequence keys. Values are ordinary table
    // constructors; arity and element types check positionally.
    
    type Pair = [string, number];
    
    local p: Pair = {"a", 1};
    local s: string = p[1];
    local n: number = p[2];
    
    // Out-of-range keys: 0 and past-the-end error, and a tuple value is a plain
    // table with no `length` field (`#t` is the operator).
    local oob = p[3];
                  ~
!!! error TLUA2493: Tuple type 'Pair' of length '2' has no element at index '3'.
    local zero = p[0];
                   ~
!!! error TLUA2493: Tuple type 'Pair' of length '2' has no element at index '0'.
    local nolen = p.length;
                    ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'length' does not exist on type 'Pair'.
    local hash: number = #p;
    
    // Element-type and arity mismatches.
    local bad: Pair = {1, "a"};
                       ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
                          ~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    local excess: Pair = {"a", 1, true};
          ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '[string, number, boolean]' is not assignable to type 'Pair'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Source has 3 element(s) but target allows only 2.
    local short: Pair = {"a"};
          ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '[string]' is not assignable to type 'Pair'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Source has 1 element(s) but target requires 2.
    
    // Optional and rest elements.
    type Trail = [string, number?, ...boolean[]];
    local t1: Trail = {"x"};
    local t2: Trail = {"x", 2, true, false};
    local restRead: boolean | nil = t2[3];
    
    // Sub-1 and non-integer keys are never elements, on every tuple shape:
    // negatives get the dedicated diagnostic (unions included), key 0 and
    // fractional keys report no-such-element/property and then resolve through
    // the number index signature like any table key.
    local vzero = t2[0];
                     ~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property '0' does not exist on type 'Trail'.
    local vneg = t2[-1];
                    ~~
!!! error TLUA2514: A tuple type cannot be indexed with a negative value.
    local pneg = p[-1];
                   ~~
!!! error TLUA2514: A tuple type cannot be indexed with a negative value.
    local pfrac = p[1.5];
                    ~~~

    local vfrac = t2[1.5];
                     ~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property '1.5' does not exist on type 'Trail'.
    declare u: [number, string] | [boolean];
    local uneg = u[-1];
                   ~~
!!! error TLUA2514: A tuple type cannot be indexed with a negative value.
    
    // An excess entry against a tuple-LIKE object with a number index signature
    // still elaborates at the entry (the index signature types the key).
    local mixed: { 1: string, [n: number]: string } = {"a", 5};
                                                            ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
!!! related TLUA6501 tluaTupleTypes.tlua:43:27: The expected type comes from this index signature.
    
    // Entries in a variadic tuple's rest part keep precise per-entry elaboration
    // (they have no property symbol, but the rest slice types them).
    local restBad: Trail = {"x", 2, true, "no"};
                                          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
    
    // A union mixing a tuple with a tuple-like interface keeps the object path,
    // so neither member's checking is lost: excess entries still error.
    interface ClosedPair {
        1: string;
        2: string;
    }
    type TupleOrLike = [string, string] | ClosedPair;
    local okUnion: TupleOrLike = {"x", "y"};
    local excessUnion: TupleOrLike = {"x", "y", "z"};
                                                ~~~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '3' does not exist in type 'TupleOrLike'.
    local wrongUnion: TupleOrLike = {"x", "y", true};
                                               ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '3' does not exist in type 'TupleOrLike'.
    
    // A non-sequence object satisfies a variadic tuple when its prefix matches
    // property-wise and every key past the prefix satisfies the rest slice;
    // elements required after the variable part are unverifiable and reject.
    declare objIn: { 1: number, 2: number };
    declare objBad: { 1: number, 5: string };
    type Vari = [number, ...number[]];
    local vOk: Vari = objIn;
    local vBad: Vari = objBad;
          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '{ 1: number; 5: string; }' is not assignable to type 'Vari'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'.
    type TrailReq = [number, ...string[], boolean];
    local vReq: TrailReq = objIn;
          ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '{ 1: number; 2: number; }' is not assignable to type 'TrailReq'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Source provides no match for required element at position 3 in target.
    
    // A union mixing a tuple with a plain (non-tuple-like) object: excess
    // entries surface the union's relation error, not a phantom-nil per-entry
    // error via the best-matching tuple constituent.
    declare mixedU: [number, number] | { kind: string };
    local mu: typeof mixedU = {1, 2, 3};
          ~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '[number, number, number]' is not assignable to type '{ kind: string; } | [number, number]'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type '[number, number, number]' is not assignable to type '[number, number]'.
!!! error TLUA2322:     Source has 3 element(s) but target allows only 2.
    
    // A SPARSE numeric-keyed union member (no key 1) also forces the object
    // path, keeping its excess-entry checking.
    interface Sparse {
        2: string;
    }
    type SparseU = [string] | Sparse;
    local sOk: SparseU = {"x"};
    local sAlso: SparseU = {"x", "y"};
    local sBad: SparseU = {"x", "y", true};
                                     ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '3' does not exist in type 'SparseU'.
    
    // Union subtype reduction: a fixed tuple is a strict subtype of the
    // exactly-in-range object (never the reverse), so the object survives --
    // deterministic and set-correct.
    declare cond: boolean;
    declare oPair: { 1: number, 2: number };
    function pickShape()
        if cond then
            local t: [number, number] = {1, 2};
            return t;
        end
        return oPair;
    end
    local picked = pickShape();
    
    // The last entry expands its pack, like every value-list tail.
    declare function two(): (number, string);
    local packed: [boolean, number, string] = {true, two()};
    
    // Inference from a table constructor through a tuple parameter.
    declare function first<A, B>(pair: [A, B]): A;
    local inferred: string = first({"x", 10});
    
    // Variadic composition and indexed access.
    type Concat<A extends unknown[], B extends unknown[]> = [...A, ...B];
    local cat: Concat<[1, 2], [3]> = {1, 2, 3};
    type Second<T extends [unknown, unknown, ...unknown[]]> = T[2];
    local second: Second<Pair> = 5;
    
    // Mapped types walk tuples element-wise with 1-based key literals.
    type Boxed<T> = { [K in keyof T]: { value: T[K] } };
    local boxed: Boxed<Pair> = { {value = "a"}, {value = 2} };
    local bv: string = boxed[1].value;
    
    // A tuple in a union context still forces the tuple literal path.
    local u: [number] | [string, string] = {"a", "b"};
